commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

commit.message:
Agent: Target State Alignment (#7499)

* Agent: Target State Alignment

* Initial plan

* Enforce formatter/parser boundary: remove dormant legacy @function source-text fallback

The formatter's printNodeDocComments previously contained a dead fallback
(lines 283-295 of print.ts) that read raw source text and converted legacy
// @function doc comment formats to /// @function, then replaced the AST's
docComments with these converted lines whenever the AST lacked an @function
tag. This was:

  - A content rewrite (format conversion) that belongs in @gml-modules/lint
    (gml/normalize-doc-comments rule), per target-state.md §2.2 and §3.2.
  - Dead code — no test fixtures exercise this path (no // @function legacy
    format fixtures exist in the test suite).
  - Superseded by the parser's normalizeFunctionDocCommentAttachments pass
    (src/parser/src/ast/normalize-function-doc-comment-attachments.ts), which
    pre-attaches recognised @function-tag comments to the correct function node
    before the formatter ever runs.

Changes:
1. print.ts: Remove the legacy @function source-text fallback block from
   printNodeDocComments. The formatter now trusts node.docComments exclusively
   (target-state.md §2.2, §3.2, §3.5).

2. function-parameter-naming-boundary.test.ts: Fix stale expected exports list
   that still included filterMisattachedFunctionDocComments (another dormant
   parser-workaround that was previously migrated out of the format workspace).
   Updated to expect only ["findEnclosingFunctionDeclaration",
   "joinDeclaratorPartsWithCommas"]. Added an explicit guard test with a comment
   documenting the migration history, so the function cannot be silently
   re-introduced (target-state.md §3.5).

3. formatter-boundaries-ownership.test.ts: Add regression-guard test
   "does not upgrade legacy double-slash @function to triple-slash" to confirm
   the formatter no longer converts // @function to /// @function. That
   conversion belongs exclusively to the lint workspace.

commit.message:
Fix parallel file corruption by grouping mutations per file (#4)

* Fix parallel file corruption by grouping mutations per file

When running with multiple workers, the Pool round-robin distributed
mutations across workers regardless of which file they targeted. This
caused multiple workers to write mutated source to the same file
simultaneously, corrupting it (Bus Error, empty files).

Now partitions mutations by file_path so all mutations for a given file
are handled by the same worker. Uses largest-first bin packing for
balanced distribution across workers.

Also adds CI timeout (5min) to prevent CI hangs from fork-based tests.

commit.message:
Refactor generate-docs into section-based architecture

Each documentation page is now driven by a section definition file in
sections/*.md. Section files contain YAML frontmatter (output filename,
title, nav_order, data_source, and per-item overrides) plus a markdown
body template with {{ placeholder }} markers for generated content.

This makes it easy to:
- Edit page copy/structure without touching Python
- Add new sections by creating a new .md file
- Override per-item descriptions in YAML frontmatter

* [fix]: fix cookie domain validation and CORS for OAuth2 login endpoints

This commit fixes the cookie domain validation error that prevented
refreshToken from being saved after Apple OAuth2 login.

**Changes**:

1. **CORS Configuration** (CorsRegistryConfig.kt)
   - Extended CORS support to /login/**, /v1/**, and /oauth2/** endpoints
   - Previously only /api/** had CORS, excluding login endpoints
   - allowCredentials=true was already configured correctly

2. **Cookie Creation** (MemberLoginController.kt)
   - createCookie() now sets domain from configuration (except localhost)
   - Sets secure flag from configuration (true for dev/prod, false for local)
   - Sets SameSite=None for cross-subdomain cookie access
   - Cookies now work correctly across subdomains

3. **Documentation**
   - Created research document for cookie domain validation issue
   - Created implementation plan for the fix
   - Added .env.example with proper cookie domain format
   - Updated AUTOMATION_GUIDE.md with cookie configuration section

**Root Cause**:
COOKIE_DOMAIN environment variable had leading dot (.depromeet.shop)
which violated RFC6265, causing Tomcat's Rfc6265CookieProcessor to
reject cookies.

**Solution**:
Code changes complete. Next step: Update CI/CD environment variables
to remove leading dot from COOKIE_DOMAIN:
  - Dev: COOKIE_DOMAIN=depromeet.shop (not .depromeet.shop)
  - Prod: COOKIE_DOMAIN=depromeet.com (not .depromeet.com)

* [fix]: add OAuth2 provider origins to CORS allowed origins

Critical fix: Apple OAuth2 callback was failing with 403 Forbidden
because the request comes from https://appleid.apple.com origin,
which was not in the CORS allowed origins list.

**Root Cause**:
When Apple redirects back with the authorization code using
response_mode=form_post, the browser submits a POST request from
https://appleid.apple.com to the callback endpoint. This request
was being rejected by Spring Security CORS because Apple's origin
was not in the allowedOrigins list.

commit.message:
Improve .NET SDK build infrastructure and documentation (#643)

* Improve .NET SDK build infrastructure and documentation

- Add Central Package Management (Directory.Packages.props) with all package versions centralized
- Add Directory.Build.props with shared properties (TargetFramework, ImplicitUsings, Nullable, TreatWarningsAsErrors)
- Add nuget.config with single nuget.org source and package source mapping (required by CPM)
- Add global.json specifying .NET 10 SDK (the library is still built with a net8.0 TFM)
- Update all CI workflows from .NET 8.0.x to .NET 10.0.x
- Enable XML documentation file generation (GenerateDocumentationFile)
- Add XML doc comments to all non-generated public types and members
- Add valid-value lists in XML docs for string properties with known values (e.g. PermissionRequest.Kind, ToolResultObject.ResultType)
- Add #pragma warning disable CS1591 to generated files (SessionEvents.cs, Rpc.cs) and codegen scripts
- Enable EmbedUntrackedSources, IncludeSymbols, SymbolPackageFormat
- Enable ContinuousIntegrationBuild conditional on CI/TF_BUILD environment variables
- Add PackageProjectUrl to package metadata
- Add [EditorBrowsable(Never)] to obsolete GithubToken property
- Upgrade analysis level and fix some diagnostics

* Exclude nodejs/scripts/ from Node.js SDK test triggers

Changes to development utility scripts (codegen, protocol version updates)
should not trigger the Node.js test suite, as they don't affect SDK runtime
code and the workflow fails on fork PRs that lack the COPILOT_HMAC_KEY secret.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Mock sendRequest in session.resume unit tests

These tests verify that the correct parameters are forwarded to the RPC
call, not that the CLI handles them. Mock sendRequest (like the setModel
test already does) so the tests don't depend on CLI authentication,
which is unavailable on fork PRs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix PermissionRequestResultKind type in custom-agents docs

Use PermissionRequestResultKind.Approved instead of string literal
"approved" to match the strongly-typed struct in the .NET SDK.

commit.message:
fix: store NULL for unset prefs so config defaults always apply

Addresses Copilot review feedback: verbose, threadedReplies, and
permissionMode had the same shadowing bug as triggerMode. The INSERT
path hardcoded defaults (verbose=0, threadedReplies=1,
permissionMode='interactive') which overrode config values once any
slash command created a prefs row.

Now all pref fields are nullable in the schema and INSERT only stores
values explicitly provided. getChannelPrefs returns undefined for unset
fields, and getEffectivePrefs falls back to config values.

feat: combined history, lazy loading, bio circuit visualization

- Combined history: GroverPOC generates run_id (crypto.randomUUID()) per run
  and sends it in both patient POST bodies. Backend saves run_id to history docs.
  History.jsx groups bio_grover_local pairs sharing the same run_id into a single
  card showing both Patient A (carrier) and Patient B (healthy) results side-by-side.

- Lazy loading: /history endpoint now accepts ?skip=N&limit=M (default 20) and
  returns {items, has_more, skip}. History.jsx uses IntersectionObserver on a
  bottom sentinel to auto-fetch the next page when scrolled into view.

- Bio circuit visualization: new GET /quantum-poc/bio-circuit-info endpoint rebuilds
  Grover step circuits from the cached NCBI sequence given target_bits, n_codons,
  has_mutation. On expand, History.jsx fetches circuits for both patients in parallel
  and renders them via the existing GroverStepNavigator component.

Fixes from adversarial review:
- groupHistory: update target_bits/marker fields when completing a pending pair
  (guard for the case where the first-seen item has null target_bits)
- build_bio_step_circuits moved to top-level import in quantum.py (was inline)

commit.message:
fix: distribute Free tier pool to Free tier nodes instead of producer

The block producer was absorbing the entire Free tier pool (8 TIME)
instead of distributing it among Free tier nodes. This caused
validation warnings (registry divergence) on other nodes since the
validator expected Free tier rewards to be shared among up to
MAX_FREE_TIER_RECIPIENTS nodes.

Aligns producer reward calculation with validator expectations:
- Producer gets 30 TIME + fees (leader bonus)
- Empty tier pools go to producer
- Paid tier pools (Gold/Silver/Bronze) go to single winner by fairness
- Free tier pool is shared among Free tier nodes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix deadlock in UDP transport detach with PJ_IOQUEUE_CALLBACK_NO_LOCK (#4832)

* Fix deadlock in UDP transport detach with PJ_IOQUEUE_CALLBACK_NO_LOCK

transport_udp_detach() holds the ioqueue key lock while calling
pj_ioqueue_clear_key(). With PJ_IOQUEUE_CALLBACK_NO_LOCK=1 (the
default), clear_key waits for read/write callbacks to finish by
checking read_callback_thread, but the callback thread needs the
same lock to set read_callback_thread = NULL. Since the lock is
recursive, clear_key's unlock only decrements the count (from 2
to 1), never fully releasing it — causing a deadlock.

The deadlock resolves after a 1-second timeout in clear_key, but
this delay causes SIPp test scenarios (e.g.
uac-inv-two-media-but-one-disabled-no-rtpmap) to fail intermittently
when a read callback happens to be active during stream destruction
on re-INVITE.

Fix by releasing the key locks after NULLing the callbacks but
before calling pj_ioqueue_clear_key(). This is safe because the
callbacks have already been set to NULL (making any in-flight
callback a no-op), and clear_key handles its own locking internally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert "fix: move AI Foundry private endpoint into Azure AI subgraph"

This reverts commit 322c74110ba50bf172e7d5f748b13fb75a358d4a.

* Remove redundant APIM Private Endpoint block from enterprise architecture

APIM with VNet injection in internal mode already provides private IP
access within the VNet. VNet injection and Private Endpoint are separate,
alternative networking models per Microsoft Learn documentation.

With VNet-injected APIM (Premium tier), clients via VPN/ExpressRoute reach
APIM directly through the subnet IP. A separate Private Endpoint is
unnecessary for this architecture.

Changes:
- Remove PEAPIM (Private Endpoint for APIM) node and edges
- Route VPN traffic directly to APIM Subnet

commit.message:
Chapter 17: Add 6 multiagent decision-making visualization components

- NormalFormGameViz: payoff matrix editor, Nash equilibria highlighting,
  dominant strategies, mixed strategy NE via KaTeX, social welfare panel
- RepeatedGameViz: iterated prisoner's dilemma with 5 FSM strategies,
  play/pause/step/speed controls, round table, limit-of-means display
- ShapleyViz: 3/4 player cooperative game editor, Shapley value with SVG
  diagram, superadditivity check, core stability, optimal coalition structure
- AuctionViz: English auction animation, Vickrey and VCG mechanisms,
  valuation sliders and revenue comparison panel
- VotingViz: 5-candidate/5-voter preference editor, plurality/Borda/IRV/
  Condorcet results, Condorcet paradox demo, strategic voting toggle
- BargainingViz: Rubinstein alternating-offers with surplus split SVG,
  Zeuthen protocol with animated negotiation rounds and risk display
- Update App.tsx to render all 6 components
